programmes.show_description: Dead Men Are Dangerous: Can Steed and the team discover the identity of the person who seemingly holds a grudge against them before it is too late?
Legacy of Death: Steed receives a jewelled dagger, known as the Falcon, whose power lies in the ability to lead its owner to a much sought after black pearl.
Hey Doc: Sgt Kimble has done his 30 years in the army and is ready to go home to open a Korean restaurant. There's only one problem, he has too much to take with him on the plane.
The Bus: Radar is driving the bus ambulance with BJ and Frank when it breaks down. No one knows which way to turn for help so they amuse themselves by recollecting their first romances.
Dear Mildred: Potter writes a letter to his wife for their anniversary. Meanwhile, Frank and Margaret try to find the perfect anniversary gift, only to be outdone by Radar.
Suspect: Matthew discovers that the neighbour's son is not as perfect as everyone seems to believe. Will Henry be forced to apologise for doubting his son?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Goodbye, Mr. Zelinka: Mr Zelinka is a beloved high-school teacher facing forced retirement because of his age. Jonathan and Mark suggest his students petition to keep him in school system.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Choices: Jonathan and Mark become private investigators for their assignment. Their clients are a Vietnamese couple trying to track down the sons they were forced to give up during the war.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Whose Trash Is It Anyway?: Mark and Jonathan join the campaign of a friend of Mark, who's highly honest. But his less than honest opponent is doing what he can to tarnish his reputation.
The Silent Bell: A minister faces a crisis of conscience and an exodus of ethnically mixed teachers and students when his church decrees that religion be taught in his preschool.
Doctor's Lady: It's love at first sight between Doc Baker and Kate Thorvald, Harriet Oleson's visiting niece. When Kate happily consents to become his wife, Doc's friends are concerned.
Plague: With an outbreak of Typhoid spreading across Walnut Grove, Charles joins forces with Doc Baker and Reverend Alden to tend to the sick and dying.
Circus Man: Jovial Willie O'Hara, a medicine salesman, comes to Walnut Grove with his 'circus'. He cures Mr Hanson's incurable headache, which gets the community talking.
The Rebellion: Grandma becomes upset when she is asked to share her role as church organist.
The Ferris Wheel: Elizabeth is found sleepwalking and having recurring nightmares about falling from a Ferris wheel. Meanwhile, Ben believes his height is affecting how people treat him.
Rock Bottom: A bizarre, ancient statue is uncovered at Dillon's farm, which seems to have a strange effect on people.
No Regrets: A radio producer causes a stir amongst the villagers when he arrives in Ormston looking for a future star. Meanwhile, Helen starts her new job as a trainee nurse.
Snowblind: Arden House is snowbound, and emotions rise to the surface as the residents spend a long night together. Dr Cameron suffers a mild heart attack while there's a power cut.
Rumpole and the Man of God: Rumpole's latest case sees him defend a vicar accused of shoplifting. But is the vicar simply trying to protect one of his parishioners?
The Kids: Nurse Cratty's orphanage is being bombed and she brings her children to the 4077th. Frank accuses one of the orphans of stealing his newly awarded Purple Heart.
Quo Vadis, Captain Chandler?: A wounded soldier tells Radar his name is Jesus Christ. Frank and Margaret think he's faking battle fatigue in order to get a discharge.
Dear Peggy: BJ is writing home to his wife Peggy that war has been called an organised bore, which reminds him of Frank Burns. Father Mulcahy gets a visit from one of his rigid superiors.

... And Amen: Poignant finale to the classic US comedy series, starring Alan Alda. In the closing days of the Korean War, the 4077 M*A*S*H Unit throw a party in the face of irrevocable changes. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

... And Amen: Poignant finale to the classic US comedy series, starring Alan Alda. In the closing days of the Korean War, the 4077 M*A*S*H Unit throw a party in the face of irrevocable changes. (1983)
Angels Of Death: The team investigate a spate of deaths involving MPs and members of the intelligence services. Evidence suggests they died of natural causes, but the team suspect foul play.
They Keep Killing Steed: An enemy agent imprisons Steed and creates a doppelganger of him to jeopardise a peace conference.
The Kids: Nurse Cratty's orphanage is being bombed and she brings her children to the 4077th. Frank accuses one of the orphans of stealing his newly awarded Purple Heart.
Quo Vadis, Captain Chandler?: A wounded soldier tells Radar his name is Jesus Christ. Frank and Margaret think he's faking battle fatigue in order to get a discharge.
Dating Henry?: Matthew is intrigued to see who his father is trying to impress when Henry plans a romantic, candlelit dinner date at home.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Summer Camp: After suffering major burns in a freak accident, a fashion model and actress becomes a counsellor at a summer camp for blind children run by Jonathan and Mark's friend Frank.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Inner Limits: A young man learns that the brother he once considered institutionalised has a brilliant mind within a paralysed body.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The Reunion: It's Mark's high school reunion. His ageing comrades from High School must come to terms with the fact that they're not the popular young people that they used to be.
The Source: A teenage girl tells school reporters that a bus driver propositioned her just before they were involved in an accident.
Child of Pain: The Walnut Grove community intervenes after Graham Stewart is found badly beaten and unconscious on the floor of his cabin. Is he the victim of drunken rage?
Money Crop: Fearing they are being swindled, angry Walnut Grove farmers vent their frustration on the wife of a man who promised them a good deal on hybrid corn.
Survival: While on their way home after a trip to Mankato, the Ingalls family are unexpectedly snowbound in a remote mountain cabin with little food.
The Elopement: Erin attempts to elope with Chad Marshall, and Jason takes over the store while Ike is away.
John's Crossroad: Due to the breakdown of the mill and mounting bills, John takes an office job in Charlottesville.
As Stars Look Down: Father Mac is visited by an old friend and former curate, now a high-flying bishop with a problem. Danny and Emma are stranded by heavy rain.
A Small Flourish: Hubert Gough is convinced that his younger wife is going to leave him when a mysterious man arrives in the village. But what is Dennis Tyldesley's real motive?
Extracurricular comedy antics with John Alderton, Deryck Guyler and Joan Sanderson. Will it be mayhem or the making of them when Mr Hedges takes the scholars of class 5C on their school trip? (1971)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Extracurricular comedy antics with John Alderton, Deryck Guyler and Joan Sanderson. Will it be mayhem or the making of them when Mr Hedges takes the scholars of class 5C on their school trip? (1971)
Of Moose and Men: Hawkeye saves the life of a colonel who wants him reprimanded for disrespect. BJ tries to save the marriage of a friend who found out his wife is cheating on him.
Soldier Of The Month: A fever of unknown origin is sending men into the hospital at an alarming rate. Frank contracts the fever just as he's about to choose the 'soldier of the month'.
The Gun: The vintage revolver of a wounded officer is stolen from the camp's gun locker and Radar is accused as the thief. Hawkeye figures out who the real culprit is.
Contemporary western starring Woody Harrelson and Liam Hemsworth. A Texas Ranger investigates murders in a mysterious frontier town, only to clash with its sinister leader. (2016)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
